repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
names_tesim: John of Mar Conon, Ma'arret Misrin (Syria)
full_text_tesim: Black and red dots, in the shape of a cross or other shapes, mark titles or new segments of text
John of Mar Conon, Ma'arret Misrin (Syria)
Most folios severely cockled, with raised ridges appearing as glossy; various degrees of discoloration, at least in part due to application of liquid in the course of prior scholarly investigation
Pencil foliation beginning with '4', intending to include exposed unnumbered front pastedown as f. 1
Quires are established according to natural movement of folios and do not correspond to original Georgian quire signatures in overtext
Reinforced leather. Large parts missing in front; back cover in two vertical parts, reattached with thread; exposed spine
The presence of quire signatures in Georgian, probably by a binder, points to the fact the manuscript was probably in Palestine between the 9th and the 10th c. CE
This is the well-known Codex Sinaiticus Syriacus
Unbound quires until f. 150; large number of singletons at the beginning and end of quires (usually quinions) that may originally have been bifolios belonging to a quire
